column1: Instructions:  Save a copy of this file with a new name in the directory hpchem\n\sequence, where n is your instrument number (typically 1),
then edit your new file in Excel or other appropriate environment to create a .CSV file for import to a ChemStation Extended Sequence Table.
Your .CSV file MUST contain a bracketed Table section identifier immediately prior to the start of the data section.  Also, fixed (permanent) column headers
must be spelled exactly as listed in this default file, unless aliases are used (see the Import CSV HELP in ChemStation for more details about aliases.)
Correct header spellings are also available from the "xtabhdr.txt" file located in the path "hpchem/ms".
Although this default CSV file contains column headers for all of the fixed (permanent) ChemStation Extended Sequence Table columns, it is not required
that your .CSV file for Extended Sequence import contain all of the fixed (permanent) Extended Sequence Table columns; any subset is also acceptable.
IMPORTANT: If you add any analysis-specific columns to your CSV file for compounds, note that the compound headers must come after the last fixed
(permanent) Extended Sequence Table header in the CSV File.
NOTE:  Rows 1 through 17 in this default file can be deleted when you prepare your own .CSV import file.  They are only for reference use.
Lines 18 and 19 can also be deleted or overwritten, but please take note of the requirement for the bracketed Table section identifier, and also the requirement for
correct column header spellings, when preparing your .CSV sequence import file.
